svg.title: Figure 2. Economic deadline and selected-channel public signal are separate diagnostics
svg.desc: Two-lane diagnostic panel for the fixed-Hero candidate hero_strong call-to-fold shift 0.25. The economic switching-opportunity lane shows T_deadline equals 100 abstract opportunities and worst-tie EV delta equals 0.000. The selected-channel public-signal lane shows showdown_reveal with reach_weighted_v1, KL 0.0, TV 0.0, and no finite estimate under the selected channel/model for T_detect.
svg.text: Figure 2. Economic deadline and selected-channel public signal are separate diagnostics
Range-matrix main row; the two scoped lanes have equal visual weight and no clock or action-advice layout.
fixed-Hero candidate row
info set: hero_strong | action label change: call -> fold | shift=0.25 | observation distance=0.25
candidate_id: IP_vs_bet::hero_strong|call->fold|shift=0.25
economic switching-opportunity diagnostic
T_deadline = 100 abstract opportunities
worst-tie EV delta = 0.000
scale: abstract-opportunity scale
This lane is an economic switching-opportunity diagnostic,
not actual adaptation time and not a physical-hand timeline.
selected-channel public-signal diagnostic
selected channel: showdown_reveal
method: reach_weighted_v1 | threshold=3.0
KL=0.0, TV=0.0 under the declared channel
T_detect = no finite estimate
under the selected channel/model
Optional neutral contrast strip, if used: losing contrast hero_chop fold->call shift=0.25 has worst diff -5.997 and no finite estimate;
larger-shift boundary hero_strong call->fold shift=1.0 has worst diff -0.855 and no finite estimate.
Caption. The range-matrix example separates an economic switching-opportunity diagnostic from a selected-channel public-signal diagnostic.
For the displayed row, T_deadline=100 on the abstract-opportunity scale. Under the declared showdown_reveal reach_weighted_v1 channel,
the same row has KL 0.0, TV 0.0, and no finite estimate under the selected channel/model for T_detect.
Production note: lane labels stay scoped to selected channel/model and abstract opportunities.
